content.text: ## Where `Unmatched dataColumnSidecars` is thrown ### 1) Range req/resp download + matching (by **slot**) **File:** `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/network/reqresp/beaconBlocksMaybeBlobsByRange.ts` **Function:** `matchBlockWithDataColumns(...)` (Fulu+ path) - **Matching mechanism:** consumes `allDataColumnSidecars` sequentially while their `signedBlockHeader.message.slot === block.data.message.slot` (slot-based grouping), assuming responses are sorted and aligned. - After iterating all blocks, it checks for **unconsumed** sidecars whose slot is still `<= endSlot`; if so it reports peer and throws: ```ts while (allDataColumnSidecars[dataColumnSideCarIndex]?.signedBlockHeader.message.slot === block.data.message.slot) { dataColumnSidecars.push(allDataColumnSidecars[dataColumnSideCarIndex]); lastMatchedSlot = block.data.message.slot; dataColumnSideCarIndex++; } // ... if ( allDataColumnSidecars[dataColumnSideCarIndex] !== undefined && allDataColumnSidecars[dataColumnSideCarIndex].signedBlockHeader.message.slot <= endSlot ) { network.reportPeer(peerId, PeerAction.LowToleranceError, "Unmatched dataColumnSidecars"); throw Error(`Unmatched dataColumnSidecars, ...`); } ``` Also inside the per-block loop it enforces that for blocks with blobs (`blobKzgCommitmentsLen > 0`) it must receive **exactly** `requestedColumns.length` sidecars and all requested indexes must be present; otherwise it throws `"Missing or mismatching dataColumnSidecars"`. **Candidate fix locations (range path):** - `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/network/reqresp/beaconBlocksMaybeBlobsByRange.ts` in `matchBlockWithDataColumns()`: - The slot-based “consume in order” logic and the final “unmatched leftovers” check are the direct source of `Unmatched dataColumnSidecars`. - If the peer can return sidecars not strictly ordered by slot, or includes extra sidecars within range, this logic will trip. --- ## Related head/chain sync and batch retry logic ### 2) Head sync / range sync batching and retry **File:** `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/sync/range/chain.ts` - `SyncChain.sendBatch()` downloads a batch via `downloadBeaconBlocksByRange` (which calls `beaconBlocksMaybeBlobsByRange`). - If download throws/errs, it calls `batch.downloadingError()` and later retries via `requestBatches()`. Key parts: - Retry selection uses `ChainPeersBalancer.bestPeerToRetryBatch(batch)` for batches in `AwaitingDownload`. - Partial downloads (missing columns) are treated as success but keep batch in `AwaitingDownload` with `pendingDataColumns`, so it will be retried with a peer that can provide remaining columns. ### 3) Pending columns tracking across retries **File:** `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/sync/range/batch.ts` - `Batch.downloadingSuccess()` merges `pendingDataColumns` across attempts by intersecting previous pending set with newly returned pending set, and stores `{blocks, pendingDataColumns}` as `partialDownload` for the next retry. - This is the mechanism that drives “batch retry for missing columns”. Relevant snippet: ```ts updatedPendingDataColumns = pendingDataColumns == null ? null : updatedPendingDataColumns.filter((column) => pendingDataColumns.includes(column)); this.state = { status: BatchStatus.AwaitingDownload, partialDownload: blocks.length === 0 ? null : {blocks, pendingDataColumns: updatedPendingDataColumns}, }; ``` ### 4) Peer selection for retries (columns-aware) **File:** `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/sync/range/utils/peerBalancer.ts` - `bestPeerToRetryBatch()` chooses peers that can satisfy the batch’s `pendingDataColumns` (or all sampled columns if no partial state yet). - For post-Fulu batches it filters peers requiring: - `earliestAvailableSlot != null` and `earliestAvailableSlot <= batch.request.startSlot` - peer custody groups intersect requested columns - Sort preference: not-failed, fewer active requests, **more matching columns**. Relevant snippet: ```ts const pendingDataColumns = partialDownload?.pendingDataColumns ?? this.custodyConfig.sampledColumns; const eligiblePeers = this.filterPeers(batch, pendingDataColumns, false); ``` --- ## “Matching is currently done (by slot)” summary - **Blocks ↔ dataColumnSidecars matching is done strictly by `slot`** in `matchBlockWithDataColumns()` by consuming sidecars in-order while sidecar.slot equals block.slot. - It assumes: 1) sidecars are grouped/sorted by slot consistently with blocks 2) no extra sidecars exist within the requested range - Violation of those assumptions triggers `Unmatched dataColumnSidecars`. --- ## Other related call sites (by-root path uses the same matcher) **File:** `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/network/reqresp/beaconBlocksMaybeBlobsByRoot.ts` - Calls `matchBlockWithDataColumns()` too (note the inline comment warning it “should not call” it for gossip/by-root because expectations differ), but the specific `Unmatched dataColumnSidecars` string is thrown only from the shared matcher in the *ByRange* file. --- ## Candidate fix touchpoints (where to investigate/change behavior) 1) **Primary:** `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/network/reqresp/beaconBlocksMaybeBlobsByRange.ts` - `matchBlockWithDataColumns()`: - slot-based sequential consumption (`dataColumnSideCarIndex` walk) - strict equality checks on `requestedColumns.length` and index presence - final leftover/unmatched sidecars check (`<= endSlot`) that emits `"Unmatched dataColumnSidecars"` 2) **Retry flow integration (if fix involves relaxing errors / treating as partial):** - `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/sync/range/chain.ts` (`sendBatch`, handling of download errors vs partial success) - `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/sync/range/batch.ts` (how partial downloads are represented and carried) - `/testbed/packages/beacon-node/src/sync/range/utils/peerBalancer.ts` (which columns the next retry will attempt to fetch) If you want, I can also enumerate the req/resp handlers for `DataColumnSidecarsByRange` and see what ordering guarantees (if any) the code assumes/implements on the response side.

+  // Instead, match by block header root and only treat sidecars as "unmatched" if they refer
+  // to a block header root we did not request.
const shouldHaveAllData = neededColumns.reduce((acc, elem) => acc && requestedColumns.includes(elem), true);

content.text: Implemented a fix for `[fulu] head sync failed due to retrying with a more up-to-date peer` by updating `matchBlockWithDataColumns()` in: - `packages/beacon-node/src/network/reqresp/beaconBlocksMaybeBlobsByRange.ts` ### What changed - Replaced **slot-based sequential consumption** of `DataColumnSidecar`s with **block-identity matching**, keyed by `block.message.bodyRoot` (same value available on `sidecar.signedBlockHeader.message.bodyRoot`). - Updated the “Unmatched dataColumnSidecars” check to only error if there are leftover sidecars that correspond to a block we actually requested; extra sidecars for a newer head block are ignored (fixes the retry-with-newer-peer case). ### Testing - Added a focused unit test to reproduce the bug, validated it passes with the fix, then **deleted the test** as required. - Re-ran an existing reqresp unit test (`collectSequentialBlocksInRange.test.ts`) to ensure nothing else regressed.

trajectory.result: Implemented a fix for `[fulu] head sync failed due to retrying with a more up-to-date peer` by updating `matchBlockWithDataColumns()` in: - `packages/beacon-node/src/network/reqresp/beaconBlocksMaybeBlobsByRange.ts` ### What changed - Replaced **slot-based sequential consumption** of `DataColumnSidecar`s with **block-identity matching**, keyed by `block.message.bodyRoot` (same value available on `sidecar.signedBlockHeader.message.bodyRoot`). - Updated the “Unmatched dataColumnSidecars” check to only error if there are leftover sidecars that correspond to a block we actually requested; extra sidecars for a newer head block are ignored (fixes the retry-with-newer-peer case). ### Testing - Added a focused unit test to reproduce the bug, validated it passes with the fix, then **deleted the test** as required. - Re-ran an existing reqresp unit test (`collectSequentialBlocksInRange.test.ts`) to ensure nothing else regressed.
